terms.plain: First-Time-Right — the pack passes all release checks on the first attempt after repair, without coming back.
Insulation resistance test — checks that high-voltage parts are electrically isolated from the metal case (safety before energizing).
Battery Management System — the pack's brain: monitors cells, controls contactors, talks to the vehicle or ESS site over CAN.
Controller Area Network — the data cable language between the battery and the vehicle/site controller. Timeouts often look like a 'dead BMS'.
State of Charge — how full the battery is, like a fuel gauge (%).
State of Health — how much capacity the pack still has vs when it was new.
Statistical Process Control — tracking measurements over time to catch drift before bad packs ship.
Eight Disciplines — structured problem-solving when the same fault keeps repeating (team → contain → root cause → fix → verify → prevent).
Non-Conformance Report — formal record when something fails spec and needs investigation.
Nickel Manganese Cobalt chemistry — higher energy, common in EV and bus packs. Sensitive to heat and cell balance.
Lithium Iron Phosphate chemistry — very stable, common in energy storage (ESS) and UPS. Long life, different voltage behaviour.
Energy Storage System — stationary battery at a site (solar, grid backup, datacenter), not in a moving vehicle.
High Voltage — typically hundreds of volts in a pack. Always treat as live until proven isolated.
Diagnostic Trouble Code — a numbered fault the BMS or vehicle stores when something goes wrong.
Bar chart of top fault types — fix the few causes that create most returns first (80/20 rule).
